title: 10 best herbal creams for periodontal disease

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Kansas coneflower cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like esculetin and esculin, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial activity: The cream's antimicrobial properties, courtesy of its sesquiterpene lactones, can inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Fusobacterium nucleatum.

Wound healing promotion: Kansas coneflower has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve wound tensile strength, and enhance tissue repair, which can aid in the treatment of gum recession and bone loss associated with periodontal disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which are common causes of periodontal disease. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Echinacea angustifolia cream may help to prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote overall oral health. This makes it a potential natural remedy for periodontal disease prevention.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease.

Alkylamides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and regulate the immune response in the body, potentially alleviating periodontal disease symptoms.

Polysaccharides: Echinacea angustifolia contains polysaccharides that may stimulate the immune system and increase its ability to fight off infections, which can help prevent the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease.
Leaves: They are a good source of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help combat the bacterial infection causing periodontal disease.
Roots: They also contain polysaccharides, which stimulate the immune system to fight against the infection and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Extract 4 ounces of dried echinacea angustifolia root in a saucepan with 8 ounces of water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of shea butter to the echinacea liquid and heat for 10 minutes.
Whisk in 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil for 2 minutes.
Pour the herbal mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antibacterial Properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial growth, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to gums and teeth.
Calendula's Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Calendula's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness associated with periodontal disease, promoting healthy gum tissue and healing.

Peppermint's Antioxidant Activity: Peppermint's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissues and teeth, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to effectively combat bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums, a key contributor to periodontal disease. By reducing bacterial loads and inflammation, tea tree creams can help prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote a healthy oral environment. Regular use of tea tree creams may also aid in the prevention of future infections and promote overall gum health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with periodontal disease.
Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to periodontal disease.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in bioactive compounds such as terpenoids, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria causing periodontal disease.
Barks: Contain salicylic acid, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in the gums.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Combine 250ml distilled water and 50g melaleuca alternifolia oil in a double boiler.
Add 100g beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted.
Mix in 10g vitamin e oil and 20g shea butter to enhance moisturizing properties.
Add 50g emulsifying wax to the mixture and stir for 5 minutes to create a smooth texture.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and harden for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue damage associated with periodontal disease.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of gum inflammation, which are characteristic of periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba extracts may have antibacterial activity against certain oral pathogens, potentially helping to prevent the development of infections that contribute to periodontal disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with periodontal disease because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba help to fight oxidative stress and promote healing in the gums, thereby reducing the risk of periodontal disease. The cream also improves blood flow to the affected areas, which aids in the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing the severity of periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help combat periodontal disease by reducing inflammation, inhibiting the growth of bacteria, and protecting against oxidative stress.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba help reduce inflammation and prevent tissue damage associated with periodontal disease by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.

Phenolic acids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): Phenolic acids in Ginkgo biloba help combat periodontal disease by exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which reduce bacterial growth and oxidative stress in the oral cavity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties beneficial for treating periodontal disease.
Seeds: The seeds contain ginkgotoxin, which has been shown to have antibacterial properties, helping to combat periodontal pathogens.
Barks: The bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree is also used, containing flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in periodontal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 30 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaves and 20 grams of vegetable oil in a container.
Combine the weighed ginkgo biloba dried leaves and vegetable oil in a double boiler and heat for 2 hours at 55-60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth and collect the infused oil in a separate container.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of emulsifying wax to the infused oil and heat until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into a mold and allow it to set and harden for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: A polyphenol compound found in turmeric, curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease.

Cineole: A monoterpene hydrocarbon found in the essential oil of turmeric, cineole has antibacterial properties that can help combat the growth of harmful bacteria like Porphyromonas gingivalis, a key contributor to periodontitis.

Ferulic acid: A phenolic compound found in turmeric, ferulic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect against tissue damage and promote wound healing in cases of periodontal disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The curcuminoids present in turmeric creams have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of infection in the gums, thereby slowing down the progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, turmeric's ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi helps to prevent the accumulation of plaque and tartar, which are major contributors to periodontal disease. This natural remedy can be a valuable adjunct to traditional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a phenolic compound, helps with periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of plaque and tartar, which are major contributors to gum disease.
Volitin: Volitin, a sesquiterpene, has antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, thereby reducing the risk of periodontal disease.

Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and preventing oxidative stress in the gums and teeth, helping to manage periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most used part is the rhyzome, which contains the active compound curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating periodontal disease.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa also contain curcumin, which is used in creams to reduce inflammation and fight bacteria that cause periodontal disease.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa are also used in creams, as they contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and protect the gums from periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Grate 500 grams of dried turmeric root into a bowl to release its active compounds.
Mix 250 milliliters of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of gelatin in a saucepan to dissolve.
Combine the gelatin mixture with 200 milliliters of coconut oil and 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the turmeric essence.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and pour it into a glass jar to cool and solidify.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Lavender's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that contribute to periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, allowing for improved wound healing and tissue repair.
Antioxidant Properties: Lavender's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissues and contribute to periodontal disease, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with periodontal disease because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in the cream, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, which are key factors in the development and progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease, promoting a healthier oral environment. This natural remedy has been used to support gum health and prevent further damage.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of biofilms, which contribute to periodontal disease by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of periodontal disease by preventing bacterial growth and reducing inflammation in the gums.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the gums and teeth from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
Flowers: The essential oils extracted from flowers possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing gum inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
Buds: The antibacterial and antifungal properties present in the buds of Lavandula angustifolia help prevent infection and promote wound healing in periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny morning when they are in full bloom.
Dry the flowers at 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Infuse 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then mix it with 200g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, which can contribute to periodontal disease by reducing the growth and spread of bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint cream, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms of periodontitis like pain, redness, and bleeding.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint cream's antioxidants help protect the mouth from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of periodontal disease by reducing collagen production and tissue degradation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The menthol in peppermint creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums, thus preventing the progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, peppermint creams stimulate blood flow to the gums, promoting healing and regeneration of damaged tissues. This natural remedy provides a potential alternative to traditional treatments for periodontal disease, making it a promising option for patients seeking a non-invasive solution.
remedy_constituents: Monoterpenes: Help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the gums, thereby mitigating periodontal disease progression.
Rosmarinic acid: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
Menthol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogens responsible for periodontitis, and providing a soothing effect on the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high amounts of menthol and menthone, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria causing periodontal disease.
Stems: The stems contain compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that aid in reducing gum inflammation and infection.
Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to prevent the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried mentha x piperita leaves and 10 grams of coconut oil for the base.
Combine the dried leaves with 50 milliliters of jojoba oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10 minutes or until the mixture reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter to the mixture and heat until fully incorporated.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial property: Ginger has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria, including those that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, thereby helping to prevent infection and reduce plaque formation.

Wound healing property: Herbal ginger creams may promote wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing inflammation, which can help to repair damaged tissues in the mouth and gum pockets associated with periodontal disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of plaque, a major contributor to periodontal disease. Additionally, ginger has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial growth and infection in the gums. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial overgrowth, ginger creams can promote healthy gum tissue and alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the gums and teeth, contributing to periodontal disease.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation, preventing bacterial overgrowth, and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Zingerone: This compound has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, prevent tissue damage, and promote wound healing in the gums, ultimately contributing to the management of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of ginger are also widely used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause periodontal disease.
Barks: The barks of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used for their antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale roots and wash them thoroughly with filtered water to remove impurities.
Chop the roots into fine pieces weighing about 20 grams and steep them in 200 milliliters of boiling water.
Strain the liquid mixture and discard the solids after 10 minutes of infusion time.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax with 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Mix the herbal infusion with 50 milliliters of distilled water and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil before blending it with the beeswax mixture.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Sage cream's antimicrobial property helps combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of sage cream reduces swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, promoting healing and tissue regeneration.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of sage cream help protect against oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can damage gum tissue and contribute to the progression of periodontal disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with periodontal disease because they contain antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and saponins, that inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums. This helps to prevent the progression of periodontal disease by reducing pocket depth and promoting the healing of damaged tissue. Additionally, sage creams can help to reduce bad breath and gum sensitivity, providing relief for those suffering from periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the bacterial load and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.

Salvianolic acid B: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the gums and surrounding tissues from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by periodontal disease.

Thujone: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the bacterial load and inflammation associated with periodontal disease, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in periodontal disease.
Stems: Contain salvinic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and protect the gums.
Roots: Used for their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 2 hours to preserve the bioactive compounds.
Steep 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 500 milliliters of carrier oil at 60°c for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the herbal extract from the oil.
Whip 200 grams of shea butter with 100 milliliters of the herbal extract and 20 grams of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation.

Antibacterial property: Pot marigold contains compounds like calophyllolide and calendulin, which exhibit antibacterial activity against various oral bacteria that can contribute to periodontal disease, promoting healthy gums and preventing infection.

Wound-healing property: Calendula's wound-healing property accelerates the process of tissue repair and regeneration, helping to restore damaged gum tissues and promote a faster recovery from periodontal surgery or treatment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's ability to reduce swelling and fight bacteria can help alleviate symptoms associated with periodontal disease, such as redness, tenderness, and bleeding gums. Additionally, Calendula's antiseptic properties can help prevent infection, promoting a healthy environment for gum tissue to heal and repair itself. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote oral health and soothe irritated gums.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial growth and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
Terpenoids: Certain terpenoids in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in periodontal tissues.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote oral health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and combat bacteria in periodontal disease.
Leaves: They have antiseptic and antifungal properties that aid in preventing infections and promoting wound healing in periodontal disease.
Seeds: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full bloom for 2-3 hours on a sunny day to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Steep 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 4 hours to create a calendula oil infusion.
Strain the oil infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then mix 1 cup of the oil with 1 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax and cocoa butter melt then remove from heat and let cool until solidified to create a calendula cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Elder cream contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to combat infection-causing pathogens in the mouth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants present in elder cream, such as anthocyanins and quercetin, can reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with periodontal disease, including swelling, redness, and pain.
Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's rich antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals, which can damage gum tissue and accelerate the progression of periodontal disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with periodontal disease because it contains bioflavonoids and other compounds that exhibit potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease. Additionally, elder creams may stimulate the production of collagen and improve the overall health of the gums, promoting a more robust barrier against bacterial invasion and reducing the risk of disease progression. This natural approach can complement conventional treatments for periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with periodontal disease.

Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in periodontal tissues, promoting healing and reducing disease progression.

Antimicrobial phenolics (e.g., rutin): These compounds demonstrate potent antimicrobial activity against various periodontal pathogens, helping to reduce the microbial load and prevent disease progression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties help combat periodontal disease.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in periodontal health.
Fruits: The berries contain antioxidants and tannins, which help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Mix the dried plant material with 100 grams of jojoba oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a warm water bath at 50-60°c for 4-6 hours with gentle stirring every 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Whip 50 grams of beeswax with the infused oil mixture to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with periodontal disease because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The cream's antibacterial properties also combat the buildup of plaque and tartar, which can lead to gum inflammation and eventually tooth loss. Additionally, sweet marjoram's antioxidant properties help protect against further damage to gum tissue, promoting a healthy and robust oral environment. By addressing these underlying issues, sweet marjoram creams provide relief from periodontal disease symptoms, allowing for improved oral health and reduced risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sweet marjoram cream contains compounds like borneol, bornyl acetate, and terpinen-4-ol that inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans, which are responsible for periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marjoram's essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and β-pinene that help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, thereby alleviating symptoms of periodontal disease.

Antioxidant properties: Sweet marjoram cream contains antioxidants like vitamin C and E, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress that can contribute to periodontal disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sweet marjoram plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing tissue damage associated with periodontal disease.
Leaves: Sweet marjoram leaves have antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial growth and reduce plaque formation, promoting healthy gums and oral tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried origanum majorana leaves and stems for extraction.
Combine the origanum majorana extract with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the infused oil and melt in a double boiler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antibacterial properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to effectively combat bacteria that cause periodontitis, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to the gums and bone. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties can help prevent secondary infections and promote healthy gum tissue growth, making it a natural and effective adjunctive therapy for managing periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, including those associated with periodontal disease, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease, such as pain and swelling.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant compounds help protect the gums and surrounding tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to periodontal disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation, making them effective in soothing gum infections.
Barks: Contain tannins and flavonoids that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in healing gums and reducing plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Blend the leaves into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Combine the paste with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler under low heat for 30 minutes to allow for infusion.
Strain and filter the cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antibacterial properties. The active compound, eugenol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum inflammation and tooth decay. By applying a clove cream directly to the affected area, it can help reduce plaque and tartar buildup, alleviate bleeding and swelling, and promote healthy gums and teeth. Additionally, clove's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated tissues and reduce discomfort associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Clove oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Tannerella forsythia, which are major contributors to periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The eugenol present in clove oil has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, making it an effective natural treatment for gum inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: Clove oil's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can damage tissues and contribute to the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like eugenol and cineole, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate periodontal disease symptoms.
Rhizomes: Contain sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality syzygium aromaticum bark and grind it into a fine powder weighing 50 grams.
Mix the powdered syzygium aromaticum with beeswax 20% and coconut oil 80% in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil 10 drops, tea tree essential oil 5 drops, and lavender essential oil 5 drops to the mixture stirring well.
Continue heating the mixture at low heat for an additional 10 minutes until it reaches a consistency similar to honey.
Remove from heat and let cool before pouring into glass jars and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with periodontal disease because they contain powerful antibacterial properties that combat the bacteria responsible for gum inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and ease discomfort, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, fennel's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gums and bone tissue, supporting overall oral health. By addressing the root causes of periodontal disease, fennel creams provide a natural and effective solution for maintaining a healthy smile.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection, reducing the risk of further damage to the gums and bone.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, allowing for more effective treatment and management of symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and lead to further progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce gum inflammation and combat bacteria responsible for periodontal disease.
Barks: The barks of the fennel plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that aid in reducing inflammation, swelling, and pain associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried fennel seeds foeniculum vulgare and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground fennel powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler set over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add 20 drops of tea tree essential oil and 15 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled and whipped cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4 degrees celsius for up to 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with periodontal disease because of their potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The creams contain dandelion extract, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation and decay. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal dandelion creams can effectively alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease, such as bleeding gums, bad breath, and loose teeth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Dandelion cream's antimicrobial activity helps combat bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis, promoting healing and tissue repair.

Wound-healing property: Dandelion's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve blood flow helps accelerate the wound-healing process, fostering a healthy environment for gums to reattach to teeth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps maintain oral health by promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, preventing periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh dandelion roots, wash them thoroughly with lukewarm water and dry in the shade for 24 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of coconut oil, 10 grams of beeswax, and 5 grams of ground taraxacum officinale in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove from heat, let cool and thicken for 1 hour before pouring into sterilized jars and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with periodontal disease because of its unique ability to soothe and calm inflamed gums. The mucilages present in slippery elm create a protective barrier that shields the gum tissue from irritation, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for reattachment of the gums to the teeth. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, allowing for more effective treatment and faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm creams contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that form a barrier on the gum tissue and reduce inflammation, providing relief from gum irritation and discomfort.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in slippery elm cream help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, promoting healthy gums and reducing oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as salicin and phenolic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain associated with periodontitis, helping to soothe and calm the affected area.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are a rich source of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the gums, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with periodontal disease.
Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and promoting healing in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried ulmus rubra bark, 20g of dried echinacea purpurea root and 10g of vitamin e oil for preparation.
Grind the dried ulmas rubra bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to ensure uniform texture.
Combine the ground ulmas rubra powder with 50ml of boiling water, allow it to steep for 30 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Mix the extracted liquid with 20g of melted beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant container, stirring until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 1 hour before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties that target swollen and irritated gums. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with periodontitis. Additionally, burdock's antibacterial properties help combat the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, promoting a healthy oral environment. This natural approach can help to reverse mild cases of periodontal disease and prevent further damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, can reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth tissues, helping to alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in burdock cream, including polyphenols and flavonoids, can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to gum tissue, which is often exacerbated by periodontal disease.

Wound-healing properties: Burdock cream's saponins and other bioactive compounds may promote wound healing by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and enhancing collagen synthesis in the gums and surrounding tissues, helping to repair damaged areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and promoting healing of gum tissue affected by periodontal disease.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of burdock contain lignans, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, helping to combat bacteria that contribute to the development of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried arctium lappa roots and chop them into small pieces.
Steep the chopped roots in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 30 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solids and obtain 250 milliliters of liquid extract.
Mix the liquid extract with 200 grams of melted coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax, stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's soothing compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to manage symptoms like bleeding and sensitivity. Additionally, chamomile's natural antibacterial agents help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and reducing plaque buildup. By addressing both inflammation and bacterial infection, chamomile creams provide a gentle yet effective treatment for periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antibacterial properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Fusobacterium nucleatum.

Wound-healing properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and essential oils promote wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing oxidative stress, which helps accelerate the repair of damaged periodontal tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing gum inflammation and soothing irritated tissues.
Barks: Chamomile bark is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, promoting healthy periodontal tissue.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers for infusion.
Combine dried flowers with cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep mixture in a warm dark place for 4 hours before straining and filtering.
Mix strained herbal infusion with beeswax pellets and vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Whisk until beeswax melts and cream thickens, then pour into sterilized containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with periodontal disease because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the mouth. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, the cream's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing in affected areas.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has antibacterial properties, which helps to reduce the growth and spread of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis, making it more comfortable for patients to undergo treatment.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and bone, reducing the risk of further disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that helps to reduce swelling and fight off infections associated with periodontal disease.
Barks: The bark of the licorice plant is used in medicinal creams for its ability to soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing pain and discomfort caused by periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 500g of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots to create the primary ingredient for the cream.
Combine 200g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 100ml of distilled water in a double boiler to infuse.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes, then strain and discard solids before cooling to room temperature.
Mix 50g of beeswax pellets and 20g of coconut oil with the cooled infusion, stirring until fully incorporated at 40°c.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers, label, and store in a cool dark place for at least 7 days before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for periodontal disease are topical preparations containing plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms and promote healing in cases of periodontal disease. These creams contain potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agents that soothe gums, reduce plaque, and prevent further infection. The best herbal creams for periodontal disease include Melaleuca alternifolia, Salvia officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Curcuma longa, Aloe vera, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea purpurea, Syzygium aromaticum, Zingiber officinale, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, periodontal disease can lead to tooth loss, bone decay, and systemic health issues, significantly impacting daily life, causing pain, embarrassment, and social withdrawal.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to periodontal disease that are treated with herbal creams are gingivitis, oral thrush, and fungal infections. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe irritated gums, reduce plaque and tartar buildup, and combat fungal growths. These natural remedies can provide relief from discomfort, bleeding, and bad breath associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with periodontal disease is a blend of Aloe Vera, Tea Tree Oil, and Echinacea. Aloe Vera reduces inflammation and soothes gums, while Tea Tree Oil's antibacterial properties combat bacteria causing the disease. Echinacea enhances the immune system, accelerating the healing process. Additionally, Neem oil can be included to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi. This combination, when used in moderation and under dental supervision, can aid in the management and treatment of periodontal disease.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of International Society of Preventive & Community Dentistry, herbal creams can be beneficial for periodontal disease due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The study, which analyzed 14 articles on medicinal plants' application in oral health care, found that various parts of these plants, such as Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ficus religiosa, and Plantago major, effectively inhibit primary plaque colonizers and periodontal pathogens. Additionally, extracts from these plants, including Moringa oleifera and pomegranate peel extract, demonstrate anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, antibacterial, and astringent actions that can help reduce gingival and periodontal diseases.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with periodontal disease because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, tea tree oil, has been shown to reduce plaque and gingivitis, common causes of periodontal disease. By inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation, tea tree oil cream can help soothe and protect the gums, promoting a healthy environment for reattachment and regeneration. Regular use may also help prevent disease progression and reduce the risk of tooth loss. Its natural properties make it a popular and effective treatment option.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Help reduce inflammation and prevent the progression of periodontal disease by inhibiting the growth of oral pathogens.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease.
Terpinen-4-ol: Exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the gums, thereby helping to combat periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antimicrobial compounds, which help combat bacterial growth and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
Barks: The barks contain various bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating periodontal conditions.
Stems: Some studies suggest that extracts from the stems of Melaleuca alternifolia may possess antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing bacterial populations in periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Melt 120g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 120g of coconut oil and 60g of shea butter in a saucepan.
Add 10g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the saucepan and stir well.
Remove the saucepan from heat and slowly pour the mixture into the melted beeswax.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and label it for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract of this herb has been found to inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, which is a primary cause of periodontal disease. Additionally, Salvia officinalis contains compounds like carnosic acid and ursolic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in the gums, thus helping to prevent further damage and promote healthy tissue regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: Terpenes have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial growth and inflammation associated with periodontal disease, reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing.

Rosmarinic acid (Phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, thereby preventing tissue damage and promoting periodontal health.

Salvianolic acid (Phenolic compound): Salvianolic acid has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat the growth of periodontal pathogens, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat periodontal disease.
Roots: Roots of Salvia officinalis contain salvinolic acids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating periodontal disease.
Barks: The barks of Salvia officinalis have been traditionally used to treat various oral health issues, including periodontal disease, due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning for optimal potency and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks or 1-2 weeks in a warm sunny location.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Whip 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil until creamy and smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's active compound, cineole, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gums, alleviate pain, and prevent further infection. It also exhibits antibacterial properties that can combat the bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus creams may help reduce plaque formation and promote a healthy oral environment, making it a valuable adjunctive therapy for periodontal disease management.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the oral cavity, thereby preventing the progression of periodontal disease.
Flavonoids: Exert antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, thus aiding in the treatment of periodontal disease.
Tannins: Exhibit antimicrobial and astringent properties, helping to reduce plaque formation, prevent bacterial growth, and alleviate gum inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are used to make creams for periodontal disease because they contain compounds like eucalyptol, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infection.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used to make creams for periodontal disease because they contain salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum disease.
Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are also used to make creams for periodontal disease because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal gum tissues.
remedy_recipe: Infuse eucalyptus globulus leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut oil 1 cup for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove leaves.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax 2 tablespoons and shea butter 2 tablespoons in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted and combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before scooping it into jars for storage and use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The curcumin in these creams reduces inflammation and promotes healing in the gums, thereby treating periodontal pockets and preventing further damage. Additionally, curcumin's antimicrobial action inhibits the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, creating an environment conducive to healthy gum regeneration and tissue repair. This results in improved gum health and reduced risk of tooth loss associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby preventing tissue damage and promoting healing.
Tumerone: It helps with periodontal disease by exhibiting antibacterial properties, which prevent the growth of harmful bacteria that contribute to the disease.
Demethoxycurcumin: It helps with periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and preventing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating symptoms and promoting gum health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are widely used due to their high content of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and prevent periodontal disease.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also used to make creams for periodontal disease, as they contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that promote oral health.

Seeds: Curcuma longa seeds are sometimes used in creams for periodontal disease, as they contain a concentrated amount of curcumin and other beneficial compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of curcuma longa powder, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Add the curcuma longa powder to the melted mixture and stir well for 2-3 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°f to 130°f.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use after 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from Aloe vera contains compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and preventing further damage. It also inhibits the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. By reducing inflammation and bacterial growth, Aloe vera creams can help to prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote a healthier oral environment. This leads to improved gum health and reduced risk of tooth loss.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: They help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which is essential for treating periodontal disease.
Glycoproteins: They exhibit antimicrobial properties, preventing the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease.
Vitamins and minerals (especially vitamin C and calcium): They help maintain the health of gums and bones, reducing the risk of periodontal disease progression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain high amounts of aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help in treating periodontal disease.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from aloe vera leaves is rich in vitamins and minerals that help soothe and heal gum inflammation and mouth ulcers associated with periodontal disease.

Juice from Leaves: The juice extracted from aloe vera leaves is known for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing plaque and preventing further gum damage in periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather all necessary ingredients including 100g of aloe vera gel 50g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax.
Melt 50g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Mix 100g of aloe vera gel with the melted coconut oil and beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools down to a creamy consistency for 10 minutes.
Pour the herbal aloe vera cream into a clean glass jar and store in the refrigerator.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with periodontal disease because of its powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It enhances blood flow to the gums, promoting healthy tissue and reducing inflammation, which is a major contributor to periodontal disease. By improving gum health and reducing bacterial growth, Ginkgo biloba creams may help prevent gum recession, reduce pocket depth, and alleviate symptoms such as bleeding gums and bad breath. This natural remedy can provide an alternative solution to traditional treatments, promoting overall oral health and well-being. Regular use may lead to improved gum health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby preventing further damage and promoting healing in periodontal disease.
Terpenoids: These lipophilic compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of free radicals, which contribute to the progression of periodontal disease.

Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which aids in the healing of damaged periodontal tissue and prevents further progression of the disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to combat periodontal disease.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in periodontal disease.

Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against periodontal disease and promote oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a clean environment for 7-10 days.
Grind 20 grams of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and 2 teaspoons of the ginkgo biloba powder into the beeswax-coconut oil mixture.
Stir and pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth can help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease, such as swelling and pain. The antimicrobial properties of Echinacea purpurea can also help combat the bacteria that cause the disease, promoting a healthier oral environment. Additionally, the cream's antioxidants can help protect the gums and mouth from oxidative stress, supporting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums associated with periodontal disease.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Echinacea purpurea have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against bacterial growth and oxidative stress in the oral cavity, contributing to periodontal disease.

Triterpene Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and reduce inflammation in the gums, potentially slowing down the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce periodontal inflammation.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkylamides that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to combat periodontal disease.
Leaves: They contain volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that have antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing the bacterial load associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to minimize moisture.
Combine 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea with 30 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax mixture over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum creams helps with periodontal disease because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, caryophyllene, in these creams has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to periodontal disease. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Syzygium aromaticum creams help to promote a healthy oral environment and prevent further progression of the disease. This can lead to improved gum health and reduced symptoms of periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and bones in periodontal disease.
Eugenol: As a phenolic compound, eugenol has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, thus preventing the progression of the disease.

β-Caryophyllene oxide: This terpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums and bones, promoting healing and preventing further damage in periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds of Syzygium aromaticum are used to make creams for periodontal disease because they contain high levels of caryophyllene, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Syzygium aromaticum are used to make creams for periodontal disease because they are rich in antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Fruits: The fruits of Syzygium aromaticum are used to make creams for periodontal disease because they contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Crush 30g of syzygium aromaticum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 30g of beeswax and 15g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60c for 15 minutes.
Add 30g of the syzygium aromaticum powder to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool to 40c before whipping with an electric mixer for 10 minutes.
Pour the whipped mixture into sterilized containers and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound, gingerol, reduces inflammation in the gums, preventing further damage and promoting healing. The cream's antimicrobial properties combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, thereby preventing the progression of the disease. Additionally, gingerol's analgesic properties alleviate pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease, making it an effective and natural remedy for maintaining oral health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and bone tissue associated with periodontal disease.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing the progression of periodontal disease and promoting oral health.

Terpenoids (specifically, bisabolol): Terpenoids, including bisabolol, have antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial infections that contribute to periodontal disease, reducing the severity of the condition and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to provide a rich source of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds that help to reduce inflammation and fight infection in the gums.
Barks: Barks are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial growth and prevent the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh zingiber officinale root, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Grate the zingiber officinale root into small pieces using a microplane grater for 2 minutes.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted oil mixture with the grated zingiber officinale root and stir for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel from the Aloe barbadensis plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth. These properties can help combat periodontal disease by reducing gum inflammation and preventing the progression of the disease. Regular use of Aloe barbadensis creams may also promote healing and prevent further damage to the gums and teeth. This can lead to healthier gums and teeth.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone constituent has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease.
Catechin: This flavonoid constituent has strong antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and prevent the formation of plaque and tartar.

Bradykininase inhibitory flavonoids: These flavonoids, including aloin and aloe-pectin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bradykinin levels, which contribute to inflammation and pain in periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel, extracted from the leaves, is used to create creams for periodontal disease due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The aloin, also extracted from the leaves, is used to make creams for periodontal disease because of its ability to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth.
Leaves: The aloe vera extract from the leaves is used to create creams for periodontal disease due to its ability to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and prepare aloe barbadensis gel by scooping 100g from a mature plant.
Combine 50g of coconut oil and 25g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 20g of vitamin e oil and 5g of distilled water to the mixture.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool to 35c.
Pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
